what_it_does: Implements a swappable speech-to-text (STT) provider layer. It normalizes incoming audio bytes to 16 kHz mono WAV, maintains a declarative registry of ears (cloud API, local HTTP services, and an in-process library), probes and reports each ear's availability, routes transcription requests by provider kind, and provides Silero VAD helpers for endpointing and barge-in detection.
observations: Uses ffmpeg to decode browser-recorded webm/opus or mp4/aac into 16 kHz mono 16-bit WAV, falling back to the original bytes if ffmpeg is missing or fails.
STT_PROVIDERS is a single registry mapping canonical ids to kind-specific specs (cloud, local_lib, local_http).
STT_ALIASES normalizes legacy names like 'whisper', 'local', and 'whisper.cpp' to canonical ids before dispatch.
available_stt() probes every registered ear and returns status details without raising; a down ear is reported as available:false with a legible reason.
transcribe() routes by kind: cloud currently only AssemblyAI, local_lib uses faster-whisper, and local_http uses stdlib-only multipart POST.
Local HTTP ears are expected to return a JSON object shaped like {"text":"..."}.
Heavy imports (faster-whisper, silero_vad) are deferred into lazy singletons so the module stays importable when they are absent.
The default ear is env-overridable via COMPANY_STT and currently defaults to whispercpp.
Secrets are read from the environment first, then from ~/company/.secrets.
Inline comments cite AGENTS.md rules, operational dates, and hardware/CUDA notes.
standouts: Registry-is-truth design: STT_PROVIDERS is the single source of which ears exist and how they are reached.
Fail-loud behavior: a selected but unreachable local_http ear raises a clear error instead of silently falling back to another provider.
Audio normalization fix: browser compressed audio is converted to WAV before transcription to avoid empty transcripts.
Lazy, guarded loading of heavy dependencies keeps the cloud path working in a lean 3.14 runtime venv.
conventions: Lazy singletons for heavy models and libraries.
Back-compat aliases plus a default alias (DEFAULT_PROVIDER = STT_DEFAULT).
Extensive inline documentation with operational rationale, dates, and hazard notes.
Registry-is-truth and fail-loud principles referenced from AGENTS.md.
concerns: _probe_provider treats any HTTP response with status < 500 as 'up', including 4xx errors, which may mask misconfigured endpoints.
_assemblyai polls for up to 90 seconds with fixed 1-second sleeps and no backoff or jitter.
_decode_to_float32 uses simple linear interpolation for resampling instead of a proper audio resampler.
_whisper_engine ignores WHISPER_DEVICE=cuda unless COMPANY_STT_FORCE_GPU is set, defaulting to CPU.
secret() re-opens and re-parses .secrets on every call with no caching.
available_stt() probes all providers on every call, including network liveness checks and import probes, which may be expensive.
transcribe_partial() re-transcribes the entire growing audio window each time, not a true incremental streaming ASR.
notes: This is the STT dispatch and availability layer: a registry-driven, fail-loud router that normalizes audio and fans out to cloud, local HTTP, or in-process Whisper ears, plus VAD helpers.
questions: What is the full runtime contract of the local_http ear services beyond returning {"text":"..."}?
How does the UI/RHM start a down local_http service using the service key in the registry?
Where is AGENTS.md located and what are the exact rules referenced throughout the comments?
How is the .voice-venv managed relative to the 3.14 runtime venv mentioned in the comments?
What should happen when _to_wav16 returns the original bytes and the ear still produces an empty transcript?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a swappable, registry-driven STT provider layer with cloud and local ears; the file matches that purpose and additionally provides VAD helpers and extensive operational commentary.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make STT provider selection, availability, and routing explicit, observable, and runtime-configurable while keeping the core module lightweight and importable across Python versions.
novelty: The combination of a single declarative ear registry with kind-based dispatch, per-provider liveness probing that never crashes the list, and mandatory audio normalization to fix browser codec mismatches before transcription.
contribution: A self-contained STT dispatch layer that contributes a provider registry, availability probing, audio normalization, kind-based transcription routing, and VAD primitives; it keeps heavy dependencies lazy and uses stdlib HTTP so the module remains importable without them.
summary_for_embedding: voice/stt.py is a registry-driven speech-to-text dispatcher that normalizes audio, probes and routes to cloud, local HTTP, or in-process Whisper providers, and exposes Silero VAD helpers.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to provide a single, observable, fail-loud STT provider layer that can switch ears via configuration without code changes.

symbols.does: Normalizes arbitrary audio bytes to 16 kHz mono 16-bit WAV using ffmpeg.
symbols.description: Runs ffmpeg in a subprocess to decode browser-recorded compressed audio and resample it to the WAV format expected by the ears. If ffmpeg is unavailable or the conversion fails, it returns the original bytes so the call does not crash.

symbols.does: Returns the currently selected default STT provider id.
symbols.description: Reads STT_DEFAULT and validates that it is a real catalog id, falling back to whispercpp if the configured default is not registered.

symbols.does: Alias of active_ear() for external payloads.
symbols.description: Provides the same default provider id under a name expected by the bridge's voice API.

symbols.does: Reads a secret from the environment or a gitignored .secrets file.
symbols.description: Checks os.environ first, then parses KEY=VALUE lines in ~/company/.secrets, returning a default empty string if the key is absent.

symbols.does: Checks whether faster_whisper is importable in the current interpreter.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util.find_spec to determine if the local library ear can actually run here, so availability reporting reflects reality.

symbols.does: Normalizes a provider id or alias to a canonical catalog id.
symbols.description: Applies back-compat aliases and raises ValueError only for ids that are genuinely absent from the registry, enforcing registry-is-truth.

symbols.does: Returns a status projection for one registered ear.
symbols.description: Checks whether a provider is usable right now based on its kind: secret presence for cloud, importability for local_lib, or HTTP liveness for local_http. Never raises; failures become available:false with a legible detail.

symbols.does: Returns the full registry as a status map.
symbols.description: Calls _probe_provider for every STT_PROVIDERS entry, producing id-to-status dictionaries that the UI/RHM can read to list ears and explain why any are unavailable.

symbols.does: Back-compat wrapper returning id-to-bool availability.
symbols.description: Derives its answer from available_stt() so the older shape cannot drift from the registry.

symbols.does: Returns a partial transcript by re-transcribing the current audio window.
symbols.description: Calls transcribe() and marks the result as partial:true. Because the registered ears are batch models, this is implemented as repeated full transcription of the growing buffer rather than true incremental streaming.

symbols.does: Transcribes a finished utterance by routing to the selected provider.
symbols.description: Resolves the provider id, normalizes audio to WAV, and dispatches by kind to cloud, local_lib, or local_http handlers. A selected-but-down ear raises loudly instead of falling back.

symbols.does: Builds a multipart/form-data body for a WAV upload.
symbols.description: Constructs the request body and Content-Type header using only stdlib modules, supporting optional extra string fields such as response_format=json.

symbols.does: POSTs audio to a local_http ear and returns the transcript.
symbols.description: Sends a multipart POST to the configured route, parses the JSON response, and returns the text. Raises a clear RuntimeError if the ear is unreachable, refusing silent fallback.

symbols.does: Small helper for urllib POST requests returning parsed JSON.
symbols.description: Wraps urllib.request to send bytes with custom headers and read back a JSON response.

symbols.does: Transcribes audio via the AssemblyAI cloud API.
symbols.description: Uploads audio, queues a transcript, polls for completion, and returns the text. Raises if the API key is missing, the job errors, or polling times out.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton for the faster-whisper model.
symbols.description: Imports faster_whisper only when needed, raising a clear install message if absent. Defaults to CPU unless COMPANY_STT_FORCE_GPU is set, and constructs the model using env tunables.

symbols.does: Decodes WAV bytes to mono float32 numpy at 16 kHz.
symbols.description: Uses soundfile to read audio, averages stereo channels, and linearly interpolates to 16 kHz for faster-whisper inference.

symbols.does: One-shot local transcription using faster-whisper.
symbols.description: Decodes the audio buffer and runs faster-whisper transcription with configured language and beam size, returning the joined segment text.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton for the Silero VAD model and utilities.
symbols.description: Imports silero_vad only when needed and caches the model plus get_speech_timestamps function for use by endpointing and barge-in logic.

symbols.does: Detects speech regions in audio for VAD purposes.
symbols.description: Returns a list of {start,end} sample regions using Silero VAD; used by the loop for endpointing and barge-in detection.

symbols.does: Convenience wrapper returning whether any speech is present.
symbols.description: Calls vad_speech_timestamps and returns true if at least one speech region was found, simplifying barge-in checks.
